crisil-ratings-press-releases.title: Securitisation deal value peaks to Rs 2.55 lakh crore in fiscal 2026
crisil-ratings-press-releases.description: The value of securitisation transactions  topped Rs 65,000 crore in January-March 2026, hoisting last fiscal’s aggregate to an all-time high of Rs 2.55 lakh crore (see Chart 1 in annexure). That translates to 20% on-year growth in the fourth quarter and 9% annually.

crisil-ratings-press-releases.title: Bank credit to grow ~13% this fiscal, a smidgen slower on-year
crisil-ratings-press-releases.description: Bank credit  is poised to grow ~13% this fiscal, driven by healthy growth in the micro, small and medium enterprise (MSME) and retail sectors, as well as the continued preference of corporates for bank credit rather than issuance of bonds amid the prevailing interest rate differential.

crisil-ratings-press-releases.title: Sunflower oil consumption set to wilt ~10% amid geopolitical heat
crisil-ratings-press-releases.description: Indian refined sunflower oil volume is poised to decline ~10% in the current fiscal, due to twin headwinds that will dampen demand. Firstly, supply-chain disruptions triggered by the Middle East conflict, and secondly, higher prices resulting from the pass-through of increasing logistics costs, will

crisil-ratings-press-releases.title: Credit ratio of India Inc moderates to 1.50 in the second half
crisil-ratings-press-releases.description: The Crisil Ratings credit ratio, or the proportion of rating upgrades to downgrades, stood at 1.50 times in the second half of fiscal 2026, moderating from 2.17 times in the first half.

crisil-ratings-press-releases.title: Cumulative recovery rate for ARCs to rise to ~90% next fiscal
crisil-ratings-press-releases.description: Asset reconstruction companies (ARCs) are expected to see the cumulative recovery rate  of security receipts (SRs) go up by ~700 basis points in the upcoming fiscal, taking the total to ~90%. Healthy performance and resolution of chunkier stressed assets in key infrastructure sectors such as real es

crisil-research.title: Regional aviation gains altitude on UDAN 2.0
crisil-research.description: The Government of India announced UDAN 2.0 (Ude Desh ka Aam Nagrik) on March 25, 2026, earmarking a capital of Rs 28,840 crore to develop 100 new airports and 200 helipads over fiscals 2027-2036. The latest regional connectivity scheme considerably builds on UDAN 1.0, which had an outlay of Rs 8,523

crisil-research.title: Crisil Economy First Cut: Keeping powder dry amid volatility
crisil-research.description: The Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) of the Reserve Bank of India (RBI) kept policy rates unchanged at its April meeting and maintained its neutral stance, giving it flexibility on future monetary policy actions.

crisil-research.title: The data transformation imperative for banks
crisil-research.description: The Fundamental Review of the Trading Book (FRTB) represents a pivotal regulatory effort to strengthen market risk management and the capital framework governing the trading activities of banks. While some jurisdictions are already in advanced stages of implementing FRTB, others have deferred or rec

crisil-research.title: Indian Economy: The fog of war
crisil-research.description: The turbulence triggered by higher tariffs levied by the United States last year did not significantly impact global macroeconomic conditions.

crisil-research.title: Global Economy: Holding up
crisil-research.description: Real gross domestic product (GDP) growth in the United States (US) eased sharply to an annualized 0.7% in the fourth quarter of 2025 from 4.4% in the third quarter

crisil-research.title: RRR: Roti Rice Rate
crisil-research.description: In March, the cost of a home-cooked vegetarian (veg) thali was flat on-year, while that of a non-vegetarian (non-veg) thali fell 1%.

ssga-insights.description: Investors often use gold tactically in their portfolios, but its function as a core diversifying asset during a variety of business cycles may demonstrate that gold can potentially play a long-term strategic role.

ssga-insights.title: Short shocks, longer echoes
ssga-insights.description: Geopolitical shocks typically spark immediate equity responses, but a disciplined, diversified approach can help navigate uncertainty toward long-term results.

ssga-insights.title: How the Alpha Meeting informs our model portfolios
ssga-insights.description: What is the Investment Solutions Group’s Alpha Meeting? And how does it factor into the investment decisions behind our model portfolios? Learn about this critical step in the model investment process.

ssga-insights.title: The yield curve's message for equity markets
ssga-insights.description: A flattening yield curve and higher real rates are lifting the bar for equities, shifting leadership toward quality, growth, and durable fundamentals.

ssga-insights.title: Fixed income scenarios for the current market environment
ssga-insights.description: Fixed income investors face significant uncertainty around the Middle East conflict. Rather than relying on a single forecast, our latest insight considers how different conflict outcomes may affect bond markets.

ssga-insights.title: Iran war complicates global policy outlook
ssga-insights.description: Central banks reacted to the Iran war with caution and hawkish tones, but markets may be overpricing inflation risks amid rising growth uncertainty.

ssga-insights.title: Private credit dispersion: Rising stress, not systemic disruption
ssga-insights.description: The rising stress in private credit highlights the importance of differentiation.

ssga-insights.title: AI meets accountability
ssga-insights.description: AI is no longer a leap of faith. Markets now demand proof, cash flow, and execution as enthusiasm gives way to selectivity.

ssga-insights.title: Iran risks support JPY; USD softens
ssga-insights.description: Escalating Iran risks lift safe‑haven demand for JPY, while the USD outlook softens amid policy uncertainty, weaker equities, and expected Fed easing.

ssga-insights.title: Investment trends among sovereign wealth funds
ssga-insights.description: Macro shifts create a tough but attractive backdrop as strong markets and policy risk push SWFs back to privates and out of fixed income.

ssga-insights.title: What to Ask Your Index Manager
ssga-insights.description: Fee compression has focused investors’ index manager selection on one important but limited criterion: price. We believe investors should be asking more questions.

ssga-insights.title: Middle East conflict: Is an end in sight by April?
ssga-insights.description: Markets are pricing a short Iran war, but history warns conflicts often drag on. A prolonged fight risks a larger energy shock, weaker growth, and sharp market repricing.

ssga-insights.title: U.S. Industrials revival: Opportunities in asset‑heavy cyclicals
ssga-insights.description: Despite macro volatility, Industrials and Materials remain undervalued and supported by strong industrial activity, manufacturing investment, and infrastructure demand.

ssga-insights.title: Iran conflict overshadows mixed global data
ssga-insights.description: Mixed US growth and inflation data take a back seat as markets focus on Iran-related oil risks, reshaping rate-cut expectations across major economies.

ssga-insights.title: Emerging Market Debt Commentary: February 2026
ssga-insights.description: EM debt gained in February as high yields and Treasuries helped returns, but late‑month geopolitical shocks saw spreads widen and the outlook turn uncertain.

ssga-insights.title: Trimming risk, staying constructive
ssga-insights.description: Rising geopolitical risk prompts modest de risking, but solid growth and Fed easing keep the outlook constructive.
